title: arctanthemum arcticum subsp. kurilense
intro: Arctanthemum arcticum subsp. kurilense, also known as the Kuril wormwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the Kuril Islands in Russia. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Arctanthemum arcticum subsp. kurilense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine in the form of poultices and salves.
